source: Ash directive 2026-06-21 (live session): 'note that pi is smart if it has faced a wall it should propose steps to fix things or use an alternate path to give its user best experience and be resourceful.'
title: Resourcefulness: no dead-ends â€” propose a fix or an alternate path when blocked
current_state: When a tool/capability is missing (observed live: 'Telegram can only send text', 'image is at this temp path'), Pi tends to report the wall and stop. The prompts don't direct Pi to route around blockers, offer alternatives, or turn a gap into a concrete next step.
target_state: Both consciousness prompts carry a RESOURCEFULNESS principle: when Pi hits a wall (missing tool, failed provider, unsupported action) it must (1) try an alternate path automatically where safe, (2) if it still can't, present the user with concrete options/next steps rather than a flat 'can't', and (3) when the gap is a real product gap, offer to file a ticket. Refusal/dead-end is the last resort, not the first response. This is behavioral guidance, not a hard tool change.
migration_plan: 1. Add a short RESOURCEFULNESS block to prompts/consciousness.txt (root) and a terse version to prompts/consciousness_normie.txt â€” keep wording consistent so both modes behave the same.
2. Phrase concretely: 'If blocked: route around it if you safely can; else give options + the best next step; never end on a bare can't. If it's a missing capability, offer to file a ticket.'
3. Pairs naturally with the output contract (T-229) â€” concise, actionable, no walls of text.
4. Verify against the T-192 consciousness tool-drift gate and T-168 ABOUT.md checks in verify.py after editing prompts.
5. Tests: testing/test_resourcefulness_prompt.py â€” both prompts contain the principle; keep it lightweight (prompt-presence assertion), since behavior itself is model-driven.
risk_notes: Prompt-only, cheap, high-leverage. Guard against over-correction: 'be resourceful' must not become 'take risky actions without asking' â€” alternate paths must still respect the existing permission gates (especially the guest execution-approval gate, T-225). Watch the consciousness drift gate in verify.py.
